title: austroeupatorium neglectum
intro: Austroeupatorium neglectum, also known as white mistflower or feverfew, is a flowering plant native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its white or pinkish flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Austroeupatorium neglectum include relief from headaches, fever reduction, and alleviation of menstrual cramps and other pain-related issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
